metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for entertainment, hobbies, leisure activities, and weekend plans

content.name: weekend
content.side_f: What do you do on the weekend? (lit. weekend you do what)
content.side_g: 周末 is a time word that functions adverbially; it typically precedes the verb without any preposition (not 在周末 in casual speech). Contrast with 末 'end' alone, which isn't used this way. Common collocations: 这个周末, 周末愉快 (have a nice weekend).

content.name: play_ball
content.side_g: 打球 is a verb-object compound; the 球 slot can be replaced or specified: 打篮球 (basketball), 打网球 (tennis). Note 踢 is used for football/soccer (踢足球), never 打. The 打 here carries a generic 'hit/strike' action meaning.

content.name: watch_look_read
content.side_a: to watch; to look; to read
content.side_g: 看 covers English 'watch/look/read' depending on the object: 看电视, 看电影, 看书, 看报. With people it means 'visit' (看朋友). Distinguish from 读 (dú, to read aloud/study) and 见 (jiàn, to meet/see someone).

content.name: television
content.side_f: I like to watch TV. (lit. electric-vision)
content.side_g: 电视 literally means 'electric-vision'. The measure word is 台 for the set (一台电视) and 个 for programs (一个电视节目). 看电视 is a fixed collocation — don't say 听电视.

content.name: sing_song
content.side_f: She really likes to sing (songs). (lit. sing song)
content.side_g: 唱歌 is a verb-object compound where 歌 is a 'dummy object' — you can insert modifiers: 唱中文歌, 唱一首歌. To say 'sing a specific song' you drop 歌: 唱这首歌. Tone sandhi: chàng gē stays 4+1.

content.name: dance
content.side_g: 跳舞 is verb-object; specific dances split it: 跳一个舞, 跳芭蕾舞 (ballet). Contrast 跳 alone (to jump) — 跳舞 is an idiomatic binome.

content.name: listen
content.side_f: I like to listen to music.
content.side_g: 听 is the general 'listen' verb, paired with 音乐, 歌, 广播 (broadcast). For 'hear' (perceive) you say 听见 or 听到 with a resultative complement. Don't confuse with 看 — you 看电视 but 听音乐 or 听歌.

content.name: music
content.side_g: 音乐 is the general noun 'music'; for 'a song' use 歌 or 歌曲. Measure word: 首 for pieces. The adjective form is 好听 (hǎotīng, pleasant to hear).

content.name: book
content.side_g: 书 takes the measure word 本 (一本书). 看书 = 'read (for leisure/study)'. Distinguish from 书法 (calligraphy) and 念书 (niànshū, to study, N. Chinese). In compounds: 书店 (bookstore), 图书馆 (library).

content.name: right_correct
content.side_g: 对 as a response particle means 'correct/right' — a softer confirmation than 是的. Also functions as preposition 'toward' (对我说) and adjective 'correct' (你说得对). Negative form is 不对.

content.name: some
content.side_f: Some people like to sing. (lit. have-some people)
content.side_g: 有的 is a determiner paired with a noun and typically appears twice in contrastive structures: 有的人…有的人… ('some...others...'). Distinguish from 一些 (a few, more quantitative) and 几个 (several, countable).

content.name: time_moment
content.side_a: (a point in) time; moment; (a duration of) time
content.side_g: 时候 is a bound noun — almost always appears in fixed frames: 有的时候/有时候 (sometimes), 什么时候 (when), …的时候 (when...). The 候 is neutral tone (shíhou). Don't confuse with 时间 (shíjiān, duration of time).

content.name: movie
content.side_f: On the weekend I'll go see a movie. (lit. electric-shadow)
content.side_g: 电影 literally 'electric-shadow'. Measure word 部 for films (一部电影) or 场 for screenings (一场电影). Fixed collocation 看电影; never 听电影. Compounds: 电影院 (cinema), 电影明星 (movie star).

content.name: often
content.side_g: 常常 is an adverb placed before the verb and after the subject: S+常常+V. Negative form is 不常 (not often), NOT 不常常. Synonym 经常 is slightly more formal/frequent in writing.

content.name: in_that_case_then
content.side_e: Nà wǒmen qù kàn diànyǐng ba.
content.side_f: In that case, let's go watch a movie.
content.side_g: 那 as a conjunction means 'in that case/then' drawing a conclusion from prior discourse — distinct from the demonstrative 那 (that). Often paired with sentence-final 吧 to make suggestions. Sometimes written 那么 for emphasis.

content.name: go
content.side_g: 去 indicates motion away from the speaker; paired with 来 (come). Structure: S+去+place (+V+O), e.g. 我去中国学中文. Unlike English, 去 doesn't take 'to' — the place follows directly. Past/completed form adds 了: 去了.

content.name: foreign_country
content.side_e: Wǒ yǒu yí ge wàiguó péngyou.
content.side_g: 外国 literally 'outside-country'. Commonly modifies nouns: 外国人 (foreigner), 外国话 (foreign language), 外国电影. More neutral than 老外 (colloquial for Westerner). Opposite 中国/本国 (one's own country).

content.name: invite_treat
content.side_a: to invite someone (to dinner, coffee, etc.); to play the host
content.side_g: 请客 is a verb-object idiom meaning 'to treat/pay the bill' in social dining — a core Chinese cultural ritual. You can split it: 请了一次客. Distinct from 请 alone ('to invite/please').

content.name: yesterday
content.side_g: 昨天 is a time word placed before the verb (typically after the subject, or topicalized sentence-initially). No preposition needed. Related: 今天 (today), 明天 (tomorrow), 前天 (day before yesterday), 后天 (day after tomorrow).

content.name: so
content.side_e: Wǒ hěn máng, suǒyǐ bú qù.
content.side_f: I'm very busy, so I'm not going.
content.side_g: 所以 marks the result clause and pairs with 因为 (because): 因为…所以… — both can appear, unlike English which drops one. 所以 always starts its own clause. Slightly more written/logical than colloquial 那/那么.
